page: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It features 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes an L3 cache of 105 MB and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for tile and integer operations. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile. The server demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, achieving a Geekbench single-core score of 2067.0 and high single-threaded compression throughput, such as 33.49 GB/sec in gzip. However, multi-core and memory-bound workloads show significant limitations. The multi-core stress-ng performance and uncached memory read bandwidth fall into the lower percentiles. Similarly, database operations and multi-core Geekbench tests are weak, reflecting the constraints of the single physical core architecture.

Positioned as a cost-efficient entry point within the Gen7 compute-optimized family, the c7i-flex.large offers a high-performance single-core profile without the cost overhead of larger multi-core instances. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight web serving, basic text processing, and utility scripting where advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 or AMX can be leveraged, while heavy multi-threaded processing, large-scale databases, and memory-intensive tasks are not recommended.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this server lacks local storage and bundled GPUs. It exhibits strong single-core performance, highlighted by high single-threaded compression speeds and a solid Geekbench single-core score. However, it faces clear tradeoffs, with weak multi-core execution and low uncached memory bandwidth. This resource profile makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for single-threaded tasks, lightweight utility scripting, and workloads that benefit from its AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets, rather than memory-intensive or highly parallelized database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 2 vCPUs, 4 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering strong single-core performance with limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 4 GB DDR5 memory, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: An entry-level compute-optimized instance delivering strong single-threaded performance and advanced Intel instruction sets for specialized workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory
Strong single-core performance in compression and synthetic benchmarks
Supports advanced vector (AVX-512) and matrix (AMX) instructions
Lacks local storage and bundled GPU resources
Best suited for single-threaded applications and lightweight utility tasks
